column1: # Downstream regression manifest — tish CONSUMERS built/tested against tish HEAD.
# Columns (TAB-separated): name  source  subdir  kind  cmd  expected
#   source:   git:URL@REF | local:PATH (dev machine only) | self:SUBDIR (a subdir of the tish checkout)
#   kind:     rust (cargo: extension/embedder) | ffi (extern-C extension) | tish (runs .tish programs)
#   expected: pass  = must build+test clean (a failure is a REGRESSION → fails the suite)
#             xfail = known-broken on this branch pending migration (green now; flags if it gets FIXED)
# For `tish` kind the runner builds the HEAD binary, `npm install`s the repo, and overwrites the
# bundled @tishlang/tish binary with HEAD so the repo's OWN test/build harness runs against HEAD.
# Run:  regression/downstream/run.sh all          # local: everything below
#       regression/downstream/run.sh --git-only   # CI: only git:/self: sources (local: skipped)
# ── ffi: extern-C extensions — the C-ABI is stable, so these MUST keep working ──
ffi-mathext
ffi-statext
tish-unity
# ── cargo: Rust extensions/embedders — the NativeFn→Callable / String→ArcStr / PropMap migration ──
# These were xfail while that migration was in flight. As of this run they build AND `cargo test`
# clean against HEAD, so they are flipped to pass (`cargo test`, not `cargo check`, to validate full
# codegen+link, not just type-check). NOTE: only tish-callbacks ships behavioral tests (3, passing);
# tish-stripe/cedar/biscuit/webauthn/brainstorm-pg have no test fns, so for them `cargo test` is a
# build/codegen guard. tish-oidc + tish-polars are still genuinely broken (kept xfail). tish-pg is a
# local path absent on this machine (skips). tish-apple-common is CONFIRMED CLEAN (pass below); its
# tish-macos/tish-ios crates are macOS-only + heavy Value-use, not Linux-checkable.
tish-pg
tish-callbacks
tish-oidc
tish-stripe
tish-cedar
tish-biscuit
tish-webauthn
brainstorm-pg
tish-polars
tish-apple
# ── tish-program consumers — no compile link; hit only by SEMANTIC changes (div-by-zero, key order) ──
# Tested via the repo's own build/test harness against the HEAD binary (see header). expected=pass —
# the language surface only GREW, so these should keep working; a RED means a real break OR a cmd that
# needs per-repo tuning (entry/feature flags). Refine the cmd as you wire each repo.
lattish
tish-ide-panels
# tish-audio / tish-midi are lattish APPS that import bare `lattish`. The runner wires the LOCAL WORKSPACE
# lattish ($TISH/../lattish) into node_modules/lattish — the npm analog of rewrite_tish_paths for the Rust
# crates — so we test against the lattish being DEVELOPED next to this tish, NOT the published package
# (which would only re-test already-released code). HEAD tish then transpiles the workspace lattish + the
# whole app (tish-audio -> 1.3 MB dist/main.js with createRoot inlined). Just `npm run build` — the runner
# does npm install + HEAD-binary override + the lattish wiring.
tish-audio
tish-learn
tish-playground
# tish-midi (deckard): a lattish APP; own npm scripts are monorepo-bound (`cd ../tish`, no `test`), so we
# transpile-smoke its JS-targetable `src/` (no native `tish:` imports; `services/` daemons excluded). The
# runner wires the LOCAL workspace lattish into node_modules (see tish-audio note), so the bare imports
# resolve to the workspace lattish.
tish-midi
column5: rc=0; while IFS= read -r f; do tish build --target js "$f" -o /tmp/_m.js >/tmp/_m.err 2>&1 || { echo "FAIL: $f"; head -2 /tmp/_m.err; rc=1; }; done < <(find src -name "*.tish" -not -path "*/node_modules/*"); exit $rc

column1: tish-tailwind
# The REAL dune-server (dune-ide headless backend) built NATIVELY against tish HEAD with its exact
# feature set — a build-regression guard for the actual server. cmd is a direct `tish build` (NOT npm),
# so the runner skips the heavy monorepo npm install. Private repo → local-only (skipped in CI). The
# always-on CI guard for the SAME primitives is the self-contained regression/dune-server suite.
dune-server
column5: TISH_HTTP_BACKEND=hyper tish build --target native --feature http,http-hyper,fs,process,ws,pty -o /tmp/_dune_server apps/dune-server/dune-server.tish

column1: spider3-tish
